payload.reason: v1→v2: two-actor plan review (Codex+Claude) both REVISE — fix incoherent mp-test invariant (1 append+1 reject, not 2), widen window via in-child monkeypatch, make .events.lock ignore product behavior (injector _CANONICAL_PATHS), tighten threading.Lock wording

v2→v3: round-2 delta review both REVISE — intent_declared self-loops (not single-fire); seed intent_declared then race plan_ready; ctx-created mp primitives; update test-local _CANONICAL_PATHS mirror + guard against leftover hand-written .state.lock

checklist.note: Matches the approved v3 plan. emit wraps validate+append in an fcntl.flock on a .harness/.events.lock sentinel (writer.py:100-125); validate moved UNDER the lock; skip_validation also holds the lock; threading.Lock kept as per-instance thread guard. Lock-sentinel ignoring promoted to product behavior via .harness/.*.lock in the injector _CANONICAL_PATHS; hand-written .state.lock line removed. d-events-append-only anchored on writer.py and reconciled (tier-2).

All changed files are within the declared scope (writer.py, gitignore_injector.py, .gitignore, d-events-append-only.md, the two test files, the plan doc). No __init__.py added — the cross-process test uses subprocess.Popen (repo idiom) rather than multiprocessing.spawn precisely to stay in scope. Attestation is the remaining scope entry.

flock critical section mirrors post_emit.py .state.lock idiom; every exit path releases (inner fd close + explicit LOCK_UN + sentinel fd close). Lock order events-before-state, never nested (verified across all emit callers). Two carry-over NITs, non-blocking: undrained p.stderr before p.wait in the subprocess test (safe — workers emit no bulk stderr; inherits the existing test_writer.py pattern); and the writer docstring references an NFS/SMB/FUSE README caveat worth confirming. Neither gates merge.

Tests non-vacuous and proven RED against pre-fix source (both race tests fail with [0,0]; the deterministic lock-scope test fails with held_during_validate=False when validation is moved outside the flock). flock conflicts across open-file-descriptions within one process (two-instances-in-one-process case), so the thread test and the NB probe are meaningful on Linux+macOS; BlockingIOError handling portable. Thread workers daemon=True + is_alive liveness assert so a lock regression fails loud, not hangs.

No AGENTS.md / cli-reference surface (no CLI verb or exit code changed); sync --check clean after gitignore managed-block regen; committed block byte-identical to _render_block(); drift guard + a new no-standalone-.state.lock guard added. writer/injector/gitignore comments accurately describe shipped behavior; test module docstring updated to describe all three tests. decision check clean, anchor hashes byte-exact.
